data.title: Gold Positive For Seventh Straight Week — Market Talk
data.description: Front-month gold futures close out another positive week, making it the seventh consecutive week they've finished higher--although front-month gold closes down for the day. For the week, gold finishes up 0.6% to $2,883.60 a troy ounce, with gains on other days offsetting the 1.5% loss the front-month contract posts today. President Trump's decision for agency heads to first study what reciprocal tariffs could be imposed depressed sentiment around gold. "This decision eased initial market jitters and led to a weaker dollar, as fears of an escalating trade war subsided-for now," says Quasar Elizundia of Pepperstone in a note. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: NEXTERA ENERGY INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
NextEra Energy, Inc. (NEE), one of North America's largest electric power and energy infrastructure companies with a strong focus on renewable energy, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces. NEE operates through two principal businesses: Florida Power & Light Company (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources (NEER), making significant strides in renewable energy and battery storage facilities.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating Revenues: $24,753 million, reflecting a decrease primarily due to the impact of non-qualifying commodity hedges and lower storm cost recovery revenues.
  • Operating Income: $7,479 million, driven by continued investments in renewable energy and battery storage facilities.
  • Net Income: $6,946 million, a decrease from the previous year primarily due to lower results at NEER and FPL, partly offset by higher results at Corporate and Other.
  • Net Income Per Share: $3.37, reflecting a decrease of $0.23 per share from the previous year due to lower results at NEER and FPL.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: NextEra Energy, Inc. (NEE) is one of the largest electric power and energy infrastructure companies in North America, with a strong focus on renewable energy. It operates through two principal businesses: Florida Power & Light Company (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources (NEER). FPL is the largest electric utility in Florida, while NEER is the world's largest generator of renewable energy from wind and solar sources.
  • Operational Capacity: As of December 31, 2024, NEE had approximately 72 gigawatts of net generation and storage capacity. This includes a diverse portfolio of natural gas, wind, solar, nuclear generation facilities, and battery storage facilities.
  • FPL Service Area: FPL serves approximately 12 million people through more than 6 million customer accounts, covering most of the east and lower west coasts of Florida and ten counties in northwest Florida.
  • NEER Renewable Energy: NEER operates facilities with a total generating capacity of approximately 41,500 MW, primarily selling capacity and energy output through long-term power sales agreements. NEER added 1,365 MW of new wind capacity, 2,507 MW of solar capacity, and 755 MW of battery storage capacity in 2024.
  • FPL Generation Sources: FPL's resources include 35,296 MW of net generating capacity, with significant contributions from natural gas, solar, and nuclear facilities. FPL also has 469 MW of battery storage capacity.
  • New Product Launches: FPL continued to expand its solar generation capacity, adding 2,235 MW in 2024 and 894 MW in January 2025, supported by the Solar Base Rate Adjustment (SoBRA) and the SolarTogether® program.
  • Geographical Performance: NEER operates across 41 states in the U.S. and four Canadian provinces, with a focus on clean and renewable energy sources. The company is a significant player in regional transmission organizations (RTOs) and independent system operators (ISOs) across North America.
  • Future Outlook: FPL plans to initiate a new base rate proceeding in 2025, proposing a general base revenue requirement increase and a Solar and Battery Base Rate Adjustment mechanism for 2028 and 2029. NEER continues to focus on expanding its renewable energy portfolio and enhancing its battery storage capabilities.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: NextEra Energy, Inc. (NEE) and its subsidiaries, including Florida Power & Light Company (FPL), are focused on expanding and enhancing their electric systems and generation facilities. NEE is investing in new wind and solar generation and battery storage facilities, with significant capital allocated to these areas. The company is also involved in the development and operation of rate-regulated transmission facilities and natural gas pipelines. NEE's strategic initiatives include increasing its backlog of contracted renewable development projects and enhancing its clean energy portfolio.
  • Capital Management: NEE's capital management activities include issuing long-term debt and equity to support its growth initiatives. In 2024, NEE issued $24.8 billion in long-term debt and $48 million in common stock/equity units. The company also engaged in the sale of independent power and other investments, generating $2.7 billion in proceeds. NEE's capital expenditures for 2024 totaled $24.7 billion, with significant investments in renewable energy projects and infrastructure. The company maintains a strong liquidity position with approximately $18.0 billion in net available liquidity as of December 31, 2024. NEE also has a program to purchase up to $150 million of common units in XPLR, with $114 million remaining under this program.
  • Future Outlook: NEE plans to continue its focus on renewable energy and infrastructure investments, with estimated capital expenditures from 2025 through 2029 aimed at further expanding its clean energy portfolio. The company intends to maintain a strong investment-grade credit rating to support its financing strategies. NEE is also exploring opportunities for additional equity and debt issuances to fund its strategic initiatives. The company is committed to maintaining a balanced capital structure to support long-term growth and shareholder value.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory and Policy Risks: NEE is heavily reliant on government policies that support clean energy, which could be adversely affected by changes in tax laws, incentives, or regulatory frameworks. The company faces competition in the Florida market for retail electricity customers, and any changes in law that increase competition could impact its business.
  • Operational and Market Risks: NEE and FPL are subject to extensive regulation, which could materially affect their business if they fail to recover costs or earn a reasonable return on investments. Regulatory decisions influenced by political and economic factors could negatively impact NEE and FPL. Changes in environmental laws could increase costs and liabilities, particularly regarding greenhouse gas emissions and nuclear operations. The company is also exposed to risks from severe weather, which can disrupt operations and increase costs. Cybersecurity threats and geopolitical factors pose significant risks to infrastructure and operations. Additionally, NEE's reliance on complex information technology systems makes it vulnerable to failures or breaches that could compromise data and operations.
  • Financial and Market Risks: NEE is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in commodity prices and interest rates, which could affect its financial performance. The company uses derivative instruments to manage these risks but acknowledges that these tools may not fully protect against significant losses. Liquidity in energy markets is crucial for managing operational risks, and any reduction in market liquidity could adversely affect NEE's business.
  • Management Strategies: Management highlights the challenges of maintaining low customer rates while ensuring reliable service amidst regulatory and market pressures. The company is focused on expanding its renewable energy portfolio but faces risks related to project siting, financing, and construction. NEE is also addressing the potential impacts of climate change on its operations and infrastructure.

SEC Filing: NEXTERA ENERGY INC [ NEE ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: EVERSOURCE ENERGY SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Eversource Energy, a leading public utility holding company, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, detailing significant improvements in financial performance, strategic initiatives, and operational highlights. The report also outlines the challenges and risks faced by the company in the evolving energy market.

Financial Highlights

Net Income/(Loss) Attributable to Common Shareholders (GAAP): $811.7 million, reflecting a significant improvement from a loss of $442.2 million in 2023, primarily due to the absence of large impairment charges recorded in the previous year.

Diluted EPS: $2.27, compared to a loss of $1.26 per share in 2023, driven by improved net income and the exclusion of significant one-time charges.

Non-GAAP Earnings: $1,634.0 million, or $4.57 per share, highlighting the company's performance excluding losses on offshore wind investments and other non-recurring charges.

Cash Flows Provided by Operating Activities: $2.16 billion, an increase from $1.65 billion in 2023, indicating stronger cash generation from core operations.

Dividends Paid: $2.86 per common share, up from $2.70 per share in 2023, reflecting the company's commitment to returning value to shareholders.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Eversource Energy operates through four main segments: electric distribution, electric transmission, natural gas distribution, and water distribution. These segments represent substantially all of Eversource's total consolidated revenues.

Geographical Performance: Eversource's electric distribution business serves customers in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire. The company also operates natural gas distribution in Massachusetts and Connecticut, and water distribution in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire.

Sales Units: As of December 31, 2024, Eversource's electric distribution segment served approximately 1.30 million customers in Connecticut, 1.58 million customers in Massachusetts, and 544,000 customers in New Hampshire. The natural gas distribution segment served approximately 315,000 customers in Massachusetts (NSTAR Gas), 332,000 customers in Massachusetts (EGMA), and 254,000 customers in Connecticut (Yankee Gas). The water distribution segment served approximately 248,000 customers across Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire.

New Product Launches: Eversource's current offshore wind business includes a noncontrolling tax equity investment in South Fork Wind, following the sale of its interests in other offshore wind projects.

New Production Launches: Eversource has continued to invest in its transmission infrastructure, with an estimated transmission rate base of approximately $10.8 billion at the end of 2024.

Future Outlook: Eversource projects a long-term EPS growth rate through 2029 in the range of 5 to 7 percent, using 2024 non-GAAP EPS as the base year. The company plans to make capital expenditures of $24.17 billion from 2025 through 2029, focusing on electric distribution, natural gas distribution, and electric transmission segments.

Operational Performance: Eversource's electric distribution segment earnings increased due to higher revenues from base distribution rate increases and capital tracking mechanisms. The electric transmission segment earnings rose due to a higher transmission rate base and the impact of the annual rate reconciliation filing with FERC. The natural gas distribution segment earnings increased due to higher revenues from base distribution rate increases and capital tracking mechanisms.

Environmental and Regulatory Compliance: Eversource is subject to various federal, state, and local environmental regulations. The company has recorded a liability for environmental remediation costs and is actively working on improving system resiliency in response to climate change.

Human Capital: As of December 31, 2024, Eversource employed 10,680 individuals, with a significant portion covered by collective bargaining agreements. The company emphasizes safety, employee engagement, and talent development as key components of its human capital strategy.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Eversource Energy has undertaken several strategic initiatives, including the sale of its offshore wind investments and the pending sale of its Aquarion water distribution business. The company completed the sale of its 50% ownership in the Sunrise Wind project to Ørsted and its 50% ownership in the South Fork Wind and Revolution Wind projects to Global Infrastructure Partners. These sales are part of a strategic shift to focus on core utility operations. Additionally, Eversource is investing in the Greater Cambridge Energy Program, which includes constructing an underground transmission substation to support decarbonization and electrification goals.

Capital Management: In 2024, Eversource issued $4.50 billion in new long-term debt and repaid $1.95 billion of long-term debt. The company also paid dividends totaling $2.86 per common share, up from $2.70 per share in 2023. Eversource completed an 'at-the-market' equity offering program, issuing 15,740,294 common shares for net proceeds of $989.4 million. The company plans to use proceeds from the pending sale of Aquarion to pay down parent company debt.

Future Outlook: Eversource projects capital expenditures of $24.17 billion from 2025 through 2029, with significant investments in electric distribution, natural gas distribution, and electric transmission segments. The company aims to achieve a long-term EPS growth rate of 5 to 7 percent through 2029, using 2024 non-GAAP EPS as the base year. Eversource plans to continue focusing on core utility operations and infrastructure improvements while managing its debt profile and maintaining its credit ratings.

Challenges and Risks

Cybersecurity Threats and Attacks: Eversource is increasingly vulnerable to sophisticated cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure, which could impair operations and damage its reputation. Despite implementing safeguards and maintaining cyber insurance, the evolving nature of cyber threats poses a significant risk.

Regulatory, Legislative and Compliance Risks: The company's earnings and liquidity are heavily influenced by regulatory decisions. Changes in rates, regulatory approvals, and compliance with environmental laws could adversely affect financial performance. Recent downgrades by S&P highlight the challenging regulatory environment in Connecticut.

Environmental and Catastrophic Events: Climate change and severe weather events pose physical and financial risks, potentially leading to significant repair costs and operational disruptions. The company must also navigate transitional impacts related to climate change, such as evolving customer expectations and workforce needs.

Market and Economic Risks: Eversource faces market risks from changes in tax laws and the potential inability to monetize investment tax credits. The company is also exposed to reputational risks due to adverse publicity and regulatory scrutiny, which could lead to increased oversight and sanctions.

Operational Risks: The company relies on third-party suppliers and service providers, which introduces risks related to substandard performance and supply chain disruptions. The loss of key personnel and challenges in hiring and retaining qualified employees could impact operations.

Financial Risks: Access to capital markets is crucial for Eversource's business strategy. Constraints on liquidity or increased borrowing costs could adversely affect financial performance. The company also faces risks related to pension obligations and potential impairments of goodwill and long-lived assets.

Management Strategies: Management is focused on addressing regulatory challenges and enhancing operational resilience. Strategies include investing in grid modernization, energy storage, and clean-energy technologies to mitigate climate change impacts. The company is also pursuing strategic workforce planning to ensure a qualified workforce.

Market Risk Management: Eversource is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in interest rates and commodity prices, which could impact financial results. The company actively manages these risks through hedging strategies and regulatory mechanisms to recover costs from customers.

SEC Filing: EVERSOURCE ENERGY [ ES ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: BRUNSWICK CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Brunswick Corporation, a leading player in the marine recreation industry, has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing the company's financial and operational performance over the past year. The report provides insights into Brunswick's strategic initiatives, business highlights, and the challenges and risks it faces in the competitive marine market.

Financial Highlights

Brunswick Corporation reported a challenging financial year in 2024, with several key metrics showing declines compared to 2023:

  • Net sales: $5,237.1 million, a decrease of 18.2% from 2023, attributed to lower wholesale ordering patterns and higher discounts.
  • Gross margin: $1,350.8 million, down 24.4% from 2023, driven by lower absorption from decreased production levels and material and labor inflation.
  • Operating earnings: $311.6 million, a significant drop of 57.6% from 2023, primarily due to lower sales and increased restructuring charges.
  • Net earnings from continuing operations: $149.3 million, a decrease of 65.5% from 2023, impacted by lower pretax income and restructuring costs.
  • Diluted earnings per common share from continuing operations: $2.21, down 63.9% from 2023, reflecting lower net earnings and share repurchases.

Business Highlights

Despite financial challenges, Brunswick Corporation achieved several operational milestones in 2024:

  • Revenue Segments: The Propulsion segment, a leader in marine engines and propulsion systems, reported net sales of $2,074.2 million. The Engine P&A segment had net sales of $1,160.8 million, while the Navico Group segment reported $800.2 million. The Boat segment, including the Brunswick Boat Group, had net sales of $1,553.5 million.
  • Geographical Performance: International sales accounted for 32% of total net sales. Europe contributed $744.4 million, Canada $275.2 million, Asia-Pacific $357.1 million, and the Rest-of-World $312.8 million. Propulsion non-U.S. sales comprised 44% of non-U.S. sales, Engine P&A 21%, Navico Group 19%, and Boat 18%.
  • Sales Units: The Propulsion segment sold engines ranging from 2.5 to 1,550 horsepower, including the Avator™ electric propulsion systems. The Boat segment's Freedom Boat Club operates in over 400 locations with approximately 60,000 memberships.
  • New Product Launches: In 2024, Brunswick launched over 100 products, including Mercury Marine's joystick piloting system and the Harris Crowne 250 pontoon. Navico Group's Simrad brand introduced the NSX ULTRAWIDE marine display, and Lowrance launched the Eagle Eye™ sonar solution.
  • New Production Launches: Mercury Marine completed solar installations in Australia and China, and the Boat Group's Venture facility in Portugal converted its heating system to natural gas.
  • Future Outlook: Brunswick plans to release its autonomous docking technology system commercially in 2025. The company aims to continue expanding its ACES strategy, focusing on autonomy, connectivity, electrification, and shared access.

Strategic Initiatives

Brunswick Corporation has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its market position and operational efficiency:

  • Management Realignment: The company realigned its management reporting and updated its reportable segments to Propulsion, Engine Parts and Accessories (Engine P&A), Navico Group, and Boat to better reflect its internal operating structure.
  • Acquisitions: Brunswick expanded its market presence and product offerings through acquisitions, including the purchase of Freedom Boat Club franchise operations and Fliteboard Pty Ltd.
  • Capital Management: In 2024, Brunswick repurchased $200 million of its common stock and paid $112.3 million in cash dividends to shareholders. The company managed its debt levels, reducing total debt to $2,340.6 million from $2,430.4 million in 2023, and maintained a debt-to-capitalization ratio of 55%.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead to 2025, Brunswick plans to reduce debt by $125 million, maintain capital expenditures at approximately $160 million, and repurchase a minimum of $80 million in shares, with potential increases if cash generation exceeds expectations.

Challenges and Risks

Brunswick Corporation faces several challenges and risks that could impact its business and financial performance:

  • Economic and Market Conditions: Economic uncertainty, inflation, and macroeconomic factors can impact consumer spending on non-essential items, affecting sales of recreational products. Currency exchange rate fluctuations, particularly a strong U.S. dollar, can adversely affect international sales and profitability.
  • Trade Policy and Tariffs: Changes in trade policies, tariffs, and import/export regulations could materially impact the company's operations and financial results, especially given the significant business conducted in international markets.
  • Supply Chain Disruptions: The company relies on third-party suppliers for raw materials and components. Supply chain disruptions, increased costs, or supplier financial difficulties could impact production and sales.
  • Climate Change and Environmental Compliance: Adverse weather conditions and climate change events can affect revenues, while compliance with environmental regulations may increase operational costs.
  • Cybersecurity and Technology Risks: The company is vulnerable to IT security incidents, which could disrupt operations and lead to financial losses. The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence technologies may intensify cybersecurity risks.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with environmental, health, safety, and other regulations could increase costs and reduce demand for products. Changes in tax laws or enforcement could also impact financial results.

SEC Filing: BRUNSWICK CORP [ BC ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Toll Brothers Announces Grand Opening of Sereno Canyon Manor Collection and Model Home in Scottsdale, Arizona
data.description:
SCOTTSDALE, Ariz., Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Toll Brothers, Inc. , the nation’s leading builder of luxury homes, today announced the grand opening of the Manor Collection at Sereno Canyon. Ideally located in North Scottsdale, Arizona, Sereno Canyon is an award-winning master-planned community of single-family homes nestled in the shadow of the McDowell Mountains. The staff-gated community features four collections offering estate-sized, single-family, and paired villa home designs ranging from 2,157 to 5,006+ square feet. The new Manor Collection’s brand-new home designs range from 4,152 to 4,655+ square feet and are priced from $2.2 million. The Toll Brothers Sales Center and Model Home are now open at 12558 East Chama Road in Scottsdale.

Located on the northeast end of the Sereno Canyon community, the Manor Collection offers 31 home sites with panoramic mountain views. The single-level home designs feature 4 to 5 bedrooms, 4.5 to 5.5 bathrooms, and 3-car garages with beautiful Desert Contemporary, Modern, and Spanish Contemporary architecture that complements the serene Sonoran Desert setting. Highlights of the incredible homes include elevated ceiling heights in the main living space, expansive great rooms with dual-sided fireplaces and multi-slide patio doors, gourmet kitchens with Wolf and Sub-Zero appliances, multigenerational living suites, and more.

“Our new Windgate Desert Contemporary model home from the Manor Collection was designed to capture the essence of indoor-outdoor living and entertaining,” said Bob Flaherty, Group President of Toll Brothers in Arizona. “The modern desert interior paired with the incredible resort-style backyard is truly one-of-a-kind, and we invite home buyers to see this stunning model home first-hand.”

Home buyers will experience one-stop shopping at the Toll Brothers Design Studio. The state-of-the-art Design Studio allows home buyers to choose from a wide array of selections to personalize their dream home with the assistance of Toll Brothers professional Design Consultants.

With 360-degree mountain views, and incredible resort amenities, Sereno Canyon offers the epitome of modern desert living. Centered at the heart of the community sits the Mountain House Lodge showcasing striking desert modern architecture with beautiful, designer-appointed features and a curated collection of art from notable local artisans. The state-of-the-art amenities include a lobby bar with four-sided fireplace for gathering, a signature restaurant with indoor and outdoor seating plus a private dining room with demo kitchen, a spa treatment room, fitness center with outdoor recreational lawn, two sparkling pools with cabanas, a private event lawn, bocce ball, and more.

Close to Scottsdale’s world-class shopping, dining, and entertainment, as well as nearby recreation, Sereno Canyon offers residents a convenient location while also providing a private setting that feels like an exclusive destination of its own.

For more information, call (844) 836-5263 or visit SerenoCanyon.com.

About Toll Brothers 

Toll Brothers, Inc., a Fortune 500 Company, is the nation's leading builder of luxury homes. The Company was founded 58 years ago in 1967 and became a public company in 1986. Its common stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “TOL.” The Company serves first-time, move-up, empty-nester, active-adult, and second-home buyers, as well as urban and suburban renters. Toll Brothers builds in over 60 markets in 24 states: Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Idaho, Indiana, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Nevada, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Oregon, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, and Washington, as well as in the District of Columbia. The Company operates its own architectural, engineering, mortgage, title, land development, smart home technology, and landscape subsidiaries. The Company also develops master-planned and golf course communities as well as operates its own lumber distribution, house component assembly, and manufacturing operations.    

In 2024, Toll Brothers marked 10 years in a row being named to the Fortune World's Most Admired Companies™ list and the Company’s Chairman and CEO Douglas C. Yearley, Jr. was named one of 25 Top CEOs by Barron’s magazine. Toll Brothers has also been named Builder of the Year by Builder magazine and is the first two-time recipient of Builder of the Year from Professional Builder magazine. For more information visit TollBrothers.com.

data.title: Twilio Slips After 4Q Adjusted Earnings, 1Q Outlook Miss
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

Shares of Twilio fell a day after the company's adjusted earnings for the fourth quarter and outlook for the first quarter missed Wall Street expectations.

The stock was down 13%, to $127.74, in midday Friday trading. Shares have jumped 18% year to date, and have more than doubled over the past 12 months.

The San Francisco communication-tools provider posted a quarterly net loss of $12.5 million, or 8 cents a share, compared with a loss of $365.4 million, or $2.01 a share, for the same period a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, the company's earnings per share came in at $1 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had forecast adjusted earnings of $1.03 a share.

Revenue rose 11%, to $1.19 billion, slightly above the $1.18 billion analysts expected.

For the first quarter, Twilio guided for revenue between $1.13 billion and $1.14 billion. Analysts expect $1.14 billion.

The company also guided for adjusted earnings per share between 88 cents and 93 cents, missing the 95 cents per share expected by Wall Street.

For the full year, Twilio expects organic revenue to grow 7% to 8%.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix

data.title: Oversupply Seen as Potential Headwind for Munis — Market Talk
data.description: Increased issuance of municipal bonds in the U.S. could become a headwind for the $4 trillion market used by local governments to finance everything, from dams to roads to airports and more. "The current oversupply [of municipal bonds] due to high debt issuance could impact their performance," says MissionSquare's Doug Cote. SIFMA data shows $512 billion in munis issued last year and $36.3 billion last month, the highest January on record. Local budgets are generally in good shape, but Cote warns that "the financial health of municipalities can vary significantly, and some may face fiscal challenges that could affect their ability to meet debt obligations." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: XERIANT, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Xeriant, Inc., a company focused on advanced materials and aerospace ventures, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal year 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities, reflecting both the challenges and opportunities faced by the company in its ongoing operations.

Financial Highlights

Net Loss: $(789,491). The decrease of $759,416 was primarily due to recording the Auctus default interest in the prior period.

Net Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.00). Weighted average number of common shares outstanding - basic and diluted was 571,876,754 for the six months ended December 31, 2024.

Business Highlights

Advanced Materials Development

Xeriant is focused on developing eco-friendly advanced materials, including the NEXBOARD™, a composite construction panel made from plastic and fiber waste. This product is designed to replace traditional materials like drywall and plywood, and the company is working on certifying it for use in the construction industry.

Aerospace Ventures

The company is exploring opportunities in aerospace, particularly in advanced air mobility and unmanned aerial systems. Xeriant aims to partner with companies to commercialize technologies that enhance sustainability and performance in aerospace.

Joint Venture Termination

The joint venture with XTI Aircraft, named Eco-Aero, LLC, was terminated on May 31, 2023. The venture was initially formed to complete the preliminary design review of XTI's eVTOL aircraft.

Legal Proceedings

Xeriant has initiated legal proceedings against XTI Aircraft Company to enforce a Letter Agreement and address alleged fraudulent acts. The company is seeking damages exceeding $500 million.

Future Manufacturing Plans

Xeriant plans to potentially set up its own manufacturing facilities for NEXBOARD™ in the United States, subject to available capital. The company is also considering licensing the technology and process.

Strategic Partnerships

The company is actively seeking partnerships and acquisitions to accelerate the commercialization of its advanced materials and aerospace technologies.

Operational Challenges

Xeriant is facing substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to ongoing net losses and a significant working capital deficit. The company is exploring options to raise additional capital.

Research and Development Focus

The company is investing in proprietary flame-retardant technology for polymers in recycled materials and is testing production processes for its NEXBOARD™ product.

Market Expansion Strategy

Xeriant is exploring licensing arrangements and joint ventures to rapidly access markets with reduced capital requirements and financial risk, leveraging partner companies' production and distribution infrastructure.

data.title: PORTSMOUTH SQUARE INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Portsmouth Square Inc., a company engaged in hotel operations, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended December 31, 2024. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, showcasing both improvements and challenges faced during the period.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $21.785 million, reflecting a slight increase compared to the previous year due to improved room and garage revenues.

Operating Income: $1.506 million for the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $217,000 for the same period in 2023, indicating improved operational efficiency.

Net Loss: $5.908 million for the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $4.126 million for the same period in 2023, primarily due to increased interest expenses.

Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share: $(8.05) for the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $(5.62) for the same period in 2023, reflecting the impact of higher net losses.

Business Highlights

Hotel Operations Performance: The company reported a net loss from hotel operations of $3,670,000 for the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to a net loss of $2,145,000 for the same period in 2023. This change is primarily due to an increase in mortgage interest expense from a 4% default additional interest rate.

Revenue Segments: For the three months ended December 31, 2024, hotel room revenues were $8,401,000, food and beverage revenues were $654,000, garage revenues were $780,000, and other operating departments contributed $130,000. Compared to the same period in 2023, food and beverage revenue decreased by $318,000, while garage revenue increased by $72,000.

Average Daily Rate and Occupancy: The average daily rate for the hotel decreased to $190 in the three months ended December 31, 2024, from $207 in the same period in 2023. However, the average occupancy increased to 88% from 81%, resulting in a flat RevPAR of $168.

Six-Month Hotel Operations: For the six months ended December 31, 2024, the hotel had operating income of $3,938,000 before interest, depreciation, and amortization on total operating revenues of $21,785,000, compared to $2,632,000 on revenues of $21,318,000 for the same period in 2023.

Six-Month Revenue Segments: For the six months ended December 31, 2024, room revenues increased by $547,000, while food and beverage revenue decreased by $212,000, and garage revenue increased by $122,000 compared to the same period in 2023.

Future Outlook: The company is actively pursuing long-term refinancing solutions and has made significant progress toward refinancing its existing debt. It is in advanced discussions with lenders and expects to complete refinancing by March 2025. The company has also completed major renovations over the past two years, with the final phase expected to be completed by the quarter ending March 31, 2025.

The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, showcasing both improvements and challenges faced during the period.

data.title: S&P 500 Touches New High; Dow Industrials, Treasury Yields Slide — WSJ
data.description:
By Katy Barnato and Owen Tucker-Smith

Stocks were mixed in Friday trading, with the S&P 500 on pace for a new high.

A day earlier, President Trump's decision to stop short of immediately imposing new tariffs, plus hopes of a resolution to the Ukraine war, had helped buoy stocks.

Notable mid-day market movers Friday include Airbnb and Roku, which both surged on the back of strong earnings reports. Coinbase shares fell even after the crypto exchange reported a surge in revenue and profit.

Meanwhile, a new report showed retail sales fell by 0.9% in January, a bigger contraction than economists had forecast.

The retail statistics came after two hotter-than-expected inflation reports earlier in the week. But many investors seem to be brushing aside the economic data, said Chris Zaccarelli, chief investment officer at Northlight Asset Management.

"You can really tell we're in a bull market," Zaccarelli said. "All bad news seems to have been overlooked, and really good news is celebrated."

In recent trading:

  • Indexes were mixed. The Dow industrials slipped, while the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite ticked up. The S&P 500 traded above the record closing high of 6118.71 that it set last month.
  • Benchmark Treasury yields ticked down. Ten-year yields sunk to about 4.48%, after retail sales fell more than expected in January.
  • Hong Kong stocks rallied further, with the Hang Seng Index rising 3.7%. Chinese President Xi will meet with Alibaba co-founder Jack Ma and other business leaders, Bloomberg and Reuters reported, raising the prospect of easing tensions between Beijing and the private sector.
  • Gold fell slightly but traded near all-time highs. The precious metal lowered to $2,897 an ounce after setting successive records.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Zoetis Gets Conditional License From USDA for Bird Flu Vaccine
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Zoetis said the U.S. Department of Agriculture's Center for Veterinary Biologics issued the company a conditional license for its Avian Influenza Vaccine, H5N2 Subtype, Killed Virus.

The vaccine is labeled for use in chickens, the company said.

The USDA's Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service said Jan. 8 it would pursue a vaccine stockpile that matches current outbreak strains. In 2016, USDA created a vaccine stockpile for use in commercial poultry, though the vaccines at that time were never deployed. Zoetis received a contract award for the 2016 stockpile.

On Friday, Zoetis said that when a new strain was identified in the U.S. in early 2022, it immediately began work to update its previous vaccine.

The company's major products include Simparica, a chewable tablet for dogs that protects against ticks and fleas. In 2024, livestock revenue was about $2.9 billion out of $9.26 billion overall.

In January, Moderna announced that a $590 million government award to support development of bird flu vaccines.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: VEECO INSTRUMENTS INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Veeco Instruments Inc., a leading manufacturer of advanced semiconductor process equipment headquartered in Plainview, New York, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the highly competitive semiconductor industry.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $717.3 million, reflecting an 8% increase from the previous year, driven by growth in the Semiconductor and Data Storage markets.

Gross Profit: $304.0 million, representing a 42% gross margin, slightly down from 43% in the prior year due to unfavorable product mix and higher service costs.

Operating Income: $67.0 million, a decrease of 4% from the previous year, impacted by increased operating expenses including R&D and asset impairment charges.

Net Income: $73.7 million, a significant improvement from a net loss of $30.4 million in the prior year, primarily due to increased sales and a favorable tax benefit.

Basic Income (Loss) Per Share: $1.31, compared to a loss of $0.56 per share in the previous year, reflecting improved profitability.

Diluted Income (Loss) Per Share: $1.23, compared to a loss of $0.56 per share in the previous year, indicating a return to profitability.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Veeco Instruments Inc. offers a range of technologies including ion beam, laser annealing, MOCVD, CVD, advanced packaging lithography, single wafer wet processing, MBE, and ALD, which are crucial in the fabrication of semiconductor devices.

Market Segments: Veeco serves four primary end-markets: Semiconductor, Compound Semiconductor, Data Storage, and Scientific & Other. The company's systems are used in the production of microelectronic components such as logic, DRAM, photonics devices, power electronics, RF filters and amplifiers, and magnetic heads for hard disk drives.

Geographical Performance: Veeco operates globally with sales and service operations across Asia-Pacific, Europe, and North America. In 2024, sales increased in China and the Rest of APAC regions, while there was a decrease in the EMEA region.

Product Lines: Key product lines include Laser Annealing Systems, Ion Beam Deposition and Etch Systems, Advanced Packaging Lithography, Single Wafer Wet Processing, MOCVD Systems, and MBE Systems. These products are integral to the company's operations and customer offerings.

New Product Launches: Veeco introduced the NSA500, a next-generation laser annealing system targeting advanced node logic and memory applications. This system complements the existing LSA product and expands the company's capabilities in nanosecond annealing.

Operational Highlights: Veeco shipped its first Nanosecond Annealing system to a leading-edge logic customer and a 300mm GaN on Si evaluation system to a Tier 1 power device customer. The company also secured multiple orders for its Wet Processing systems from a leading foundry and HBM manufacturer.

Future Outlook: Veeco anticipates growth in leading-edge investments driven by new nodes and AI-related demand, including Gate-All-Around nodes, High-Bandwidth Memory, and 3D packaging for AI. However, engagement with customers in China has moderated, and a decline in China revenue is expected heading into 2025.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Veeco Instruments Inc. has been actively investing in research and development to maintain its competitive advantage in the semiconductor and compound semiconductor markets. The company has focused on expanding its Served Available Market (SAM) by developing new technologies such as the NSA500 laser annealing system and Ion Beam Deposition systems. These initiatives are aimed at capturing growth opportunities in advanced node logic and memory applications, as well as expanding into new applications like Gate-All-Around transistors and advanced 3D devices.

Capital Management: Veeco has managed its capital through a combination of debt and equity activities. The company has $26.5 million outstanding principal balance of 3.50% convertible senior notes due in 2025, which were settled through the issuance of company shares upon maturity. Additionally, Veeco has $25.0 million of 3.75% convertible senior notes due in 2027 and $230.0 million of 2.875% convertible senior notes due in 2029. The company also maintains a $225.0 million revolving credit facility, which remains undrawn. In terms of equity, Veeco has not paid dividends on its common stock, and future dividend policy will be determined based on various financial factors.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Veeco anticipates growth driven by investments in new nodes and AI-related demand, including Gate-All-Around nodes and High-Bandwidth Memory. However, the company expects a decline in revenue from China in 2025 due to moderated customer engagement. Veeco plans to continue its strategy of investing in advanced logic and memory technologies to outperform Wafer Fabrication Equipment growth and expand its SAM. The company also aims to manage its capital efficiently to support these strategic initiatives and maintain financial flexibility.

Challenges and Risks

Market and Operational Risks: Veeco operates in highly cyclical industries, such as Semiconductor and Data Storage, which are subject to rapid technological changes and evolving market demands. This requires continuous innovation and adaptation to maintain competitiveness. The company's reliance on a concentrated customer base and limited geographic regions increases exposure to local economic and political challenges.

Regulatory and Geopolitical Risks: The company faces significant market risks due to unfavorable market conditions, which can lead to reduced demand, increased competition, and supply chain disruptions. Operating a global business exposes the company to geopolitical tensions, trade policy changes, and regulatory challenges, particularly with U.S.-China relations affecting export controls and tariffs. The semiconductor industry's rapid technological advancements necessitate substantial R&D investments, posing risks if the company fails to predict market trends accurately.

Operational Risks: Operational risks include reliance on a limited number of suppliers, which could lead to supply chain interruptions. The company's outsourcing strategy, while aimed at cost efficiency, may result in operational difficulties if third-party providers fail to perform as expected. The long and unpredictable sales cycle, coupled with the potential for order cancellations, adds to the volatility of the company's financial performance.

Regulatory and Cybersecurity Risks: Regulatory risks are heightened by the need to comply with diverse global regulations, including data privacy, anti-corruption, and environmental laws. The company is also exposed to cybersecurity threats, which could lead to significant financial and reputational damage if sensitive information is compromised.

Emerging Risks: Emerging risks include the impact of AI and machine learning technologies, which, while offering opportunities, also pose operational and competitive challenges. The company's success is contingent on effectively managing these technologies and mitigating associated risks.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by market volatility and geopolitical tensions, emphasizing the need for strategic resource allocation and innovation to sustain growth. The company is focused on enhancing its competitive position through technological advancements and expanding its customer base.

Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect its financial results. Hedging activities are employed to mitigate these risks, but they may not always be sufficient to offset potential impacts. Additionally, tightening credit markets could affect customer purchasing power and supplier stability, further influencing the company's financial performance.

data.title: CANADA ECONOMICS FEATURE: CIBC's Avery Shenfeld On the Surplus the U.S. Runs with Canada
data.description:
According to CIBC's Avery Shenfeld in his 'The Week Ahead' column, it's unlikely that the facts about U.S. trade relationships are going to be read by today's oval office resident. But, perhaps, he said, those surrounding President Trump can still be influenced by the data. "If so," Shenfeld added, "Canadian diplomatic efforts in Washington should be calling attention to the surplus that the U.S. currently runs with Canada — yes, a surplus — and how that ties into the overall shape of America's balance of payments with other countries."

Shenfeld wrote: "The surplus we're referencing is the flip side of America's trade deficit with Canada, in the so-called financial account. In the past four quarters, Canadians have invested C$176 billion more in U.S. assets than the flow from the US into Canada [all figures used are in Canadian dollars]. About a quarter of that gap is in foreign direct investment, where Canadians have poured nearly $80 billion over four quarters into American projects against a flow about half that size the other way. Some of those are just the sort of ribbon cutting events that Donald Trump likes to trumpet as a sign of America's success, when he can stand for a photo-op with a Japanese or European CEO.

"The largest driver of the US surplus on investment flows is in in debt securities, where Canada's net buying of $148 billion in U.S. bonds and money market instruments dwarfs the $64 billion coming northbound into Canada's debt markets. And that, in fact, is part of the broader story on why the US runs a trade deficit with the rest of the world.

"The US is fifth on the list of the 10 richest countries in the OECD in terms of per capita income at purchasing power exchange rates. Its rapid growth in the post-pandemic period, outstripping most of its trading partners, has drawn in more imports as its consumers outspent those in other countries. That alone would have pushed its trade deficit wider, a mark of its economic success, not failure.

"But some of that growth has come from stimulative fiscal policy, with the U.S. running much larger budget deficits as a share of GDP, and from households' willingness to have a relatively low savings rate. Measured in terms of total net borrowing for the government sector (at all levels), IMF data show that the US ran the largest government financing need in the G-7, at 7.6% of GDP. That compares to Canada at 2% of GDP.

"The result leaves the U.S. dependant on capital inflows to finance not only its government sector, but the private capital spending in an economy that has been booming relative to many of its counterparts. Those capital inflows, and the resulting financial account surplus, push the US dollar to a level that, in turn, makes American exports less competitive, and drives an offsetting current account and trade deficit. Remember that the balance of payments has to, in fact, balance. If the US draws in more of the worlds' savings and investment flows, the buying of U.S. dollars for that surplus has to line up with the net selling of U.S. dollars for goods and services trade (and investment income flows).

"Instead of tariffs, the U.S. could improve its trade balance by repositioning its economy to draw in less of those investment flows, thereby weakening the greenback. To do that, it might have to tax its citizens more (it has a lower tax burden than most OECD countries), cut government spending, and thereby reduce its need for foreign borrowing. With a less-stimulative fiscal policy, it could have lower interest rates that are less of a magnet for the foreign capital inflows that keep the US dollar elevated, and the weaker greenback would improve its trade balance.

"Or it could simply choose to ignore those trade balances, and recognize that to some extent, they are a sign of a successful economy that has its consumers spending a lot on imports, and foreigners eager to invest in U.S. dollar assets, not just to fund the deficit, but as in Canada's case, undertake foreign direct investment in new plants, buildings and hard assets as a way of joining in on America's success.

"Mr. President, despite the trade gap, America is already pretty great."

data.title: State of Wisconsin Investment Board reports over $321M BTC exposure
data.description:
The State of Wisconsin Investment Board, the entity overseeing the state’s pension fund, increased its Bitcoin exposure to around $321 million, according to a Feb. 14 filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission.

Wisconsin’s pension fund previously disclosed holding $164 million in Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) in a May 2024 SEC filing.

At the time, the pension fund held approximately 2.4 million shares of BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), valued at $100 million, and 1 million shares of Grayscale’s Bitcoin Trust (GBTC), valued at $64 million.

The pension fund’s most recent filing indicates that the fund has allocated all of its BTC exposure into IBIT and no longer holds any shares of GBTC.

The State of Wisconsin Investment Board’s increased Bitcoin exposure highlights a growing trend among pension funds allocating a portion of their assets to Bitcoin as a hedge against currency inflation and a diversified portfolio.

Pension funds adopt Bitcoin to protect purchasing power

Although Bitcoin is notorious for its high volatility, pension funds have a long time horizon for their investments that allows them to capture long-term price appreciation while ignoring short-term price movements.

Attorney Allie Itami of Lathrop GPM told Cointelegraph that state pension funds could adopt crypto more easily than privately managed funds due to fiduciary restrictions under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) of 1974.

Following Wisconsin’s May 2024 filing disclosing BTC exposure, several state pension funds also disclosed BTC ETF holdings.

In July 2024, the State of Michigan Retirement System reported $6.6 million in BTC exposure, which is only a tiny fraction of the multibillion-dollar fund’s assets under management.

Florida chief financial officer Jimmy Patronis wrote a letter to the Florida State Board of Administration — the state’s pension manager — urging the fund to allocate a portion of its assets to BTC in October 2024.

More recently, in February 2025, North Carolina House of Representatives speaker Destin Hall introduced a bill to allow the state treasury to invest in digital assets indirectly by holding ETFs.

data.title: Daily Roundup of Key US Economic Data for Feb. 14
data.description:
Retail sales fell by 0.9% in January and were down 0.4% excluding a 2.8% drop in auto sales, below estimates for both metrics.

A 4.6% drop in sporting goods sales and a 1.7% drop in furniture sales were the key factors, offset by sales gains at restaurants and department stores. Control group retail sales, which exclude motor vehicles, gas, building materials, and food services, fell by 0.8% after a 0.9% gain in December.

Import prices rose by 0.3% in January and were still up 0.1% excluding a 3.2% gain in fuel prices. Petroleum prices were up 2.9%. Export prices increased by 1.3% in January and were up 1.5% excluding a 0.2% decline in agricultural prices.

January industrial production rose by 0.5% after a 1% gain in December, with manufacturing production down 0.1% overall but up 0.2% excluding a 5.2% drop in motor vehicle and parts production. Utilities output surged by 7.2%, lifted by the demand for heat due to cold weather. Mining production fell by 1.2%.

Business inventories fell by 0.2% in December, below expectations and following a 0.1% increase in November. Business sales rose by 0.8%, with gains at the factory, wholesale, and retail levels.

The Q1 GDPnow estimate from the Atlanta Fed is for a 2.3% gain, revised down from a 2.9% gain reported on Feb. 7. The next update is scheduled for Feb. 19.

The St. Louis Fed's initial GDP nowcast estimate for Q1 is for a 2.11% gain.
